title: Lansium decandrum
intro: Lansium domesticum, also known as the Lansium or Langsat, is a tropical fruit-bearing tree native to Southeast Asia. The Langsat tree has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate fever, reduce oxidative stress, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of the Langsat fruit include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with the Langsat fruit include tea, juice, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.

The species is also known as the "Asam Kasturi" or "Malaysian cherry" in local languages. Lansium decandrum is closely related to other species in the Lansium genus, which includes Lansium domesticum and Lansium parvifolium.
